comparisons.name: Senior CommUnity Care of Kentucky (PACE)
comparisons.dataShapeNotes: County-restricted to Jefferson and Northern KY counties only; two regional providers with separate contacts; no income/asset dollar limits specified; fees for non-dually eligible; nursing home level of care certification required
gotchas: Not statewide—only specific counties; confirm residence[1][2][5]
Medicare-only or private pay participants face fees/restrictions, not 100% covered like dually eligible[3]
All non-emergency care must go through PACE providers to be covered[3]
Enrollment makes PACE the sole source of services—no duplication with other programs[5]
Must be able to live safely in community at enrollment time, despite nursing home level needs[1][2][3]

comparisons.name: Kentucky Family Caregiver Program (Grandparents Raising Grandchildren)

diffs.found: Wide range of services including matching with support groups, information about resources, assistance accessing services, counseling, and training. Eligible participants may receive a grant or voucher per grandchild per fiscal year for clothing, respite, school supplies, educational needs, required legal services, medical/dental services, and other approved expenses (specific dollar amounts not stated).[6][9]

novelFields.note: Our model has no asset limit fields

novelFields.value: Administered through local Area Agencies on Aging and Independent Living (e.g., Pennyrile[1], KIPDA region: Jefferson, Oldham, Henry, Trimble, Spencer, Shelby, Hardin[2]). Variations in local providers and potentially services/wait times.
novelFields.note: Program varies by region — our model doesn't capture this

value: Proof of relationship, residency, household income, grandchild's age, confirmation parents do not live in home, and no Kinship Care (inferred; not explicitly listed).
novelFields.note: Has document checklist — our model doesn't store per-program documents

comparisons.dataShapeNotes: Administered statewide via local Area Agencies on Aging; financial aid requires 150% FPL income test and no Kinship Care overlap; benefits include non-cash services plus discretionary grants/vouchers per fiscal year.
gotchas: Cannot receive benefits if already getting Kinship Care payments ('double dip' rule); evaluate which program is better.[1][4][6]
Parents cannot reside in the household, even if not primary caregivers.[1][6][9]
Income limit (150% FPL) applies strictly to financial assistance; services like support groups may be more accessible.[1][4][6]
Adopted grandparents may still qualify under certain conditions.[7]
